Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, with low urine specific gravity and significant microalbuminuria, indicating possible severe renal impairment or nephrotic syndrome. Urine is cloudy, brown, and contains ketones and bilirubin, but no infection markers. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
